title: Load-based mapping
abstract: This document describes techniques and apparatuses for load-based mapping. The techniques provide routes for drivers that take into account a cognitive, emotional, or psychological load for the routes. By so doing, a driver can receive a route from one point to another that the driver may better enjoy, feel less tired or stressed by, or take less mental effort to drive.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: determining, based on one or more factors affecting a cognitive, emotional, or psychological load, and a time or distance for potential routes from a first location to a second location, a determined route including the first location to the second location; and providing the determined route to a driver of the route. 2. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the determining the determined route determines a change to a portion of an existing route, the change to the portion altering the existing route based on the one or more factors. 3. The method as recited in claim 1 , wherein determining the determined route determines that a prior route driven multiple times by the driver advances the driver from the first location to the second location, and wherein determining the determined route includes using the prior route. 4. The method as recited in claim 3, wherein using the prior route increases a total time for the route. 5. The method as recited in claim 3, wherein the determined route indicates directions to the beginning of the prior route or from the end of the prior route to the second location. 6. The method as recited in claim 5, wherein the providing the determined route ceases to provide audio instructions during the prior route. 7. The method as recited in claim 3, wherein the providing the determined route provides directions from an end of the prior route. 8. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the one or more factors affecting the cognitive, emotional, or psychological load includes a road-surroundings narrowness, a number of lanes, an amount of nature, an amount of industry, types of vehicles, a perceived danger, a prior-driven route or route portion, or a number of route-based disturbances. 9. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein determining the determined route is further based on previously determined preferences of the driver. 10. The method as recited in claim 1 , wherein determining the determined route further comprises receiving multiple potential routes and determining, based on the factors and the time or distance, the determine route from the received multiple routes. 11. A computing device comprising: an audio or visual output structure; an input structure; one or more processors; and one or more computer-readable storage memory having one or more instructions stored thereon that, responsive to execution by the one or more processors, causes the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: receiving, through the input structure, a request for a route to an end location from a start location; determining, based on one or more factors affecting a cognitive, emotional, or psychological load, and a time or distance for potential routes from start location to the end location, a determined route from the start location to the end location; and providing, through the audio or visual output structure, the determined route to a driver of the route or an entity from which the request was received. 12. The computing device of claim 11, wherein the one or more factors affecting the cognitive, emotional, or psychological load includes a road-surroundings narrowness, a number of lanes, an amount of nature, an amount of industry, types of vehicles, a perceived danger, a prior-driven route or route portion, or a number of route-based disturbances. 13. The computing device of claim 11, wherein determining the determined route is further based on preferences or a history of the driver. 14. The computing device of claim 11, wherein determining the determined route further comprises receiving multiple potential routes and determining, based on the factors and the time or distance, and the determining selects the determine route from the received multiple routes. 15. The computing device of claim 11, wherein determining the determined route does not receive potential routes. 16. The computing device of claim 11, wherein the determining the determined route determines a change to a portion of an existing route, the change to the portion altering the existing route based on the one or more factors. 17. The computing device of claim 11, wherein determining the determined route determines that a prior route driven multiple times by the driver advances the driver from the first location to the second location, and wherein determining the determined route includes using the prior route. 18. The computing device of claim 17, wherein using the prior route increases a total time for the route. 19. The computing device of claim 17, wherein the determined route indicates directions to the beginning of the prior route or from the end of the prior route to the second location. 20. The computing device of claim 17, wherein the providing the determined route provides directions from an end of the prior route.
